commit.message:
fix(testhub): fall back to committed packages when API returns 403

The GITHUB_TOKEN in CI lacks read:packages scope, causing ListPackages to
return a 403 error. Previously this silently wrote 0 packages to testhub.json,
breaking the package table E2E test and rendering an empty testhub page.

When package listing fails, loadFallbackTesthubPackages() reads the committed
src/data/testhub.json and reuses its package list. This preserves real data
(from seed or prior successful runs) while still updating build count history.

This mirrors the homebrew fallback pattern (stats-latest.json cache restore).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct CI verify step false-positive + add no-empty-state E2E tests

Root cause: grep -c 'chart-empty' dist/index.html matched the CSS class
definition .chart-empty[data-astro-cid-xxx] inside Astro's inlined <style>
tag, producing a false positive on every healthy build. This caused 4
consecutive failed deploys on commits that were actually correct.

Fix 1 (CI): Change grep pattern to 'class="chart-empty"' — matches only
actual rendered HTML elements, not CSS definitions. Also extend check to
all 3 pages (index, testhub, overall), not just index.html.

Fix 2 (E2E): Add 3 new Playwright tests under 'No empty charts contract'
that use page.locator('.chart-empty') to detect any chart in empty state
across all 3 pages. These tests catch the failure class that the CI step
was supposed to catch but couldn't due to the false-positive bug.

Fix 3 (AGENTS.md): Document 'Definition of Done' — CI must be green before
a task is declared complete. Local tests are necessary but not sufficient.

21 E2E tests now passing (was 18).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Optimize generic-assay-meta endpoints with ClickHouse derived table (#11979)

* perf: optimize generic-assay-meta with ClickHouse derived tables and DDD architecture

Replace N+1 loop + 3-step SQL chain with pre-computed ClickHouse derived
tables and a single query. Reduces O(N) SQL queries to 1 (0 on cache hit)
for multi-profile requests. Introduces column-store DDD architecture for
the generic-assay-meta endpoint.

ClickHouse schema:
- Add generic_assay_meta_derived table with pre-aggregated Map(String, String) properties
- Add generic_assay_profile_entity_derived table for fast profile-entity resolution
- Add ClickhouseMapTypeHandler for Map column type mapping
- Bump derived table version from 1.0.7 to 1.0.8

Column-store DDD layer:
- Add GetGenericAssayMetaUseCase with @Cacheable and TreeSet-based cache key normalization
- Add ColumnStoreGenericAssayController at /api/column-store/generic-assay-meta/*
- Add single getGenericAssayMetaByProfileIds query joining derived tables
- Keep legacy GenericAssayServiceImpl and GenericAssayController unchanged

Streaming and injection:
- Switch GenericAssayController to StreamingResponseBody for lower memory usage
- Convert GenericAssayController and GenericAssayServiceImpl to constructor injection

Tests:
- Add ColumnStoreGenericAssayControllerTest and GetGenericAssayMetaUseCaseTest
- Add ClickhouseMapTypeHandlerTest and ClickhouseGenericAssayMapperTest
- Add 5 branch-coverage tests to GenericAssayServiceImpTest
- Update GenericAssayControllerTest for StreamingResponseBody async dispatch

Add support for singleton tables (#123)

* Add implementation plan for singleton table support

Documents the design decisions and implementation steps for #[dsl(singleton)]
attribute that enables 0-1 row tables with auto-injected #[primary_key] id: ().

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VH7cwCHrweaWEfBz37iphC

* rm `id` column type check for error

* Implement singleton table support for #[dsl(singleton)]

Adds the `singleton` keyword to the `#[dsl]` attribute macro, enabling
single-row tables with auto-injected `#[primary_key] id: u8` column.

Key changes:
- Parse `singleton` flag in DSL attribute args
- Auto-inject `#[primary_key] id: u8` as first struct field
- Generate dedicated get/update/delete methods without `_by_id` suffix
- Skip get_all/count_of_all method generation for singletons
- Skip per-column CRUD methods and getter/setter for injected PK
- Validate: no #[index], #[unique], #[referenced_by], plural_name,
  unique_index, or multiple #[table] attrs on singletons
- Support FK columns on singletons without requiring an index
- Handle FK cascade for singletons using .id().find(&0u8)
- Add singleton test example (GameConfig)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VH7cwCHrweaWEfBz37iphC

* Address review feedback on singleton table support implementation (#124)

* Initial plan

* Refactor: merge singleton get/update/delete into GetOne/Update/DeleteOne

Remove the three dedicated singleton functions (for_singleton_get,
for_singleton_update, for_singleton_delete) and instead modify the
existing GetOne, Update, and DeleteOne primary-key method generation
to handle singleton tables inline via an is_singleton_pk flag.

Changes:
- SpacetimeDSLColumnMethods::map(): remove blanket early-return for
  singletons; only non-unique (multi-row) indexes are skipped for
  singletons now.
- for_method(): add is_singleton_pk detection after index_columns are
  populated; use it to adjust doc_comment, trait_name, and method_name
  so singletons get e.g. get_game_config() instead of
  get_game_config_by_id().
- Update arm: inject `entity.id = 0u8` before the update call for
  singletons to prevent callers from setting an incorrect id.
- GetOne/DeleteOne column loop: guard method_args.push and
  take no arguments.
- GetOne false branch: hardcode find(&0u8) path for singletons.
- DeleteOne arm: add singleton early branch using id().delete(&0u8)
  with full hook support but without the wrapper-type assumption that
  would panic for plain-u8 pk columns.
- Remove for_singleton_get, for_singleton_update, for_singleton_delete.
- Remove singleton generation block from try_parse.
- Remove get_singleton/update_singleton/delete_singleton from
  SpacetimeDSLTableMethods struct and all usage sites.

commit.message:
fix: use set:html for chart data injection; add Playwright E2E tests

- Replace set:text with set:html on all <script type="application/json"> elements
  set:text HTML-encodes quotes to " which browsers do NOT decode inside
  <script> raw text elements, causing JSON.parse() to fail silently for
  every chart on the site. set:html injects verbatim content instead.

- Add Playwright E2E chart regression suite (18 tests, 3 pages):
  * Verifies all 9 JSON data payloads parse as valid JSON in-browser
  * Verifies all canvas elements have non-zero bounding boxes (Chart.js rendered)
  * Regression guards for key contracts (lowercase distros, title-case osVersionDist)
  * Verifies countme explainer callout is visible

- Add vitest.config.ts to exclude tests/e2e from unit test runner
- Add just test-e2e and just test-all targets to Justfile
- Wire E2E tests into CI (npx playwright install + npm run test:e2e)
  with failure artifact upload for playwright-report/

Root cause: Astro set:text is correct for HTML elements but wrong for
<script> elements because browsers treat script content as raw text (no
entity decoding). The fix applies to all 9 chart components across 3 pages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(agents): better handling of agents spawning sub-agents

- Extract child agent tracking to separate module (agent_child_tracking.py)
- Add child agent tracking to AgentRegistry (parent→children mapping)
- Track active children and their last activity timestamps
- Make SDK timeout logic child-aware to prevent premature timeouts
- Parent agents won't timeout while active children are producing output
- Clean up child tracking on agent removal
- Add comprehensive tests for child agent relationship tracking

This resolves issues where parent agents would timeout waiting for
background sub-agents (e.g., spawned via task tool) because the parent
appeared idle despite active child work.

Also updates ruff config to allow print() statements in desktop UI
and SDK helper code where they provide user feedback.
